steps.title: Identify the key relationship
steps.content: Gruen conceived the mall as a communal gathering space for strolling and lingering. The second sentence then describes his actual design with promenades, seating, fountains, and greenery. The design is a direct consequence of his original vision.

steps.title: Match the signal
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-and-effect: Gruen's vision of an indoor town square caused him to design the mall with pedestrian-friendly features. The blank needs a transition word signaling that the design choices follow logically from the vision.
  • Choice A — "Regardless,": signals that something happens despite an opposing force, so it fails because no obstacle is being overcome here. ✘
  • Choice B — "By contrast,": signals opposition, so it fails because the design aligns with, not contradicts, Gruen's communal vision. ✘
  • Choice C — "Accordingly,": signals that what follows is a logical consequence of what came before, so it fits — the promenades and seating directly result from his vision of a communal strolling space. ✓
  • Choice D — "In addition,": signals a new parallel point, so it fails because the design is not a separate fact alongside the vision — it is the vision put into practice. ✘
That matches choice C — C is correct.
